title: planchonella thiensis
intro: Planchonella thiensis, also known as the Red Bead Tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, including Fiji and Samoa. The medicinal properties of Planchonella thiensis include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing fever, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function.
